projectDescription: An open-source machine learning compiler that optimizes models from PyTorch, TensorFlow, and JAX for high-performance execution across GPUs, CPUs, and ML accelerators.
keyFeatures: Multi-framework support: integrates with PyTorch, TensorFlow, and JAX via StableHLO
Cross-platform execution: targets GPUs (NVIDIA, AMD), CPUs, and custom ML accelerators
Automated optimization passes: hundreds of HLO passes including fusion, algebraic simplification, and rematerialization
PJRT uniform device API: hardware-agnostic runtime interface for ML frameworks
XLA FFI custom calls: stable ABI for integrating external kernels and libraries
Pluggable backend architecture: extensible compiler for new hardware targets
Megascale distributed training: multi-host SPMD support for large model parallelism
Triton and LLVM code generation: high-performance GPU and CPU kernel generation
publicApiSurface: XlaOp — handle for HLO operations in the builder
PjRtClient — uniform device API for compilation and execution (xla/pjrt/pjrt_client.h)
PjRtExecutable — compiled program ready for execution
XLA FFI (xla/ffi/) — foreign function interface for custom calls
HloModulePass — base class for HLO optimization passes (xla/hlo/pass/hlo_pass_interface.h)
StableHLO — public input IR (versioned MLIR dialect)
run_hlo_module — CLI tool to run and test HLO modules
hlo-opt — CLI tool for running individual HLO passes
multihost_hlo_runner — CLI tool for SPMD multi-host HLO execution
